83 class InventoryModule(BaseInventoryPlugin, Cacheable):
84 ''' Host inventory parser for ansible using foreman as source. '''
85
86 NAME = 'foreman'
87
88 def 
init
(self):
89
90 super(InventoryModule, self).
init
()
91
92 # from config
93 self.foreman_url = None
94
95 self.session = None
96 self.cache_key = None
97 self.use_cache = None
98
99 def verify_file(self, path):
100
101 valid = False
102 if super(InventoryModule, self).verify_file(path):
103 if path.endswith(('foreman.yaml', 'foreman.yml')):
104 valid = True
105 else:
106 self.display.vvv('Skipping due to inventory source not ending in "foreman.yaml" nor "foreman.yml"')
107 return valid
108
109 def _get_session(self):
110 if not self.session:
111 self.session = requests.session()
112 self.session.auth = HTTPBasicAuth(self.get_option('user'), to_bytes(self.get_option('password')))
113 self.session.verify = self.get_option('validate_certs')
114 return self.session
115
116 def _get_json(self, url, ignore_errors=None):
117
118 if not self.use_cache or url not in self._cache.get(self.cache_key, {}):
119
120 if self.cache_key not in self._cache:
121 self._cache[self.cache_key] = {'url': ''}
122
123 results = []
124 s = self._get_session()
125 params = {'page': 1, 'per_page': 250}
126 while True:
127 ret = s.get(url, params=params)
128 if ignore_errors and ret.status_code in ignore_errors:
129 break
130 ret.raise_for_status()
131 json = ret.json()
132
133 # process results
134 # FIXME: This assumes 'return type' matches a specific query,
135 # it will break if we expand the queries and they dont have different types
136 if 'results' not in json:
137 # /hosts/:id dos not have a 'results' key
138 results = json
139 break
140 elif isinstance(json['results'], MutableMapping):
141 # /facts are returned as dict in 'results'
142 results = json['results']
143 break
144 else:
145 # /hosts 's 'results' is a list of all hosts, returned is paginated
146 results = results + json['results']
147
148 # check for end of paging
149 if len(results) >= json['subtotal']:
150 break
151 if len(json['results']) == 0:
152 self.display.warning("Did not make any progress during loop. expected %d got %d" % (json['subtotal'], len(results)))
153 break
154
155 # get next page
156 params['page'] += 1
157
158 self._cache[self.cache_key][url] = results
159
160 return self._cache[self.cache_key][url]
161
162 def _get_hosts(self):
163 return self._get_json("%s/api/v2/hosts" % self.foreman_url)
164
165 def _get_all_params_by_id(self, hid):
166 url = "%s/api/v2/hosts/%s" % (self.foreman_url, hid)
167 ret = self._get_json(url, [404])
168 if not ret or not isinstance(ret, MutableMapping) or not ret.get('all_parameters', False):
169 ret = []
170 return ret.get('all_parameters')
171
172 def _get_facts_by_id(self, hid):
173 url = "%s/api/v2/hosts/%s/facts" % (self.foreman_url, hid)
174 return self._get_json(url)
175
176 def _get_facts(self, host):
177 """Fetch all host facts of the host"""
178
179 ret = self.
get_facts_by_id(host['id'])
180 if len(ret.values()) == 0:
181 facts = {}
182 elif len(ret.values()) == 1:
183 facts = list(ret.values())[0]
184 else:
185 raise ValueError("More than one set of facts returned for '%s'" % host)
186 return facts
187
188 def to_safe(self, word):
189 '''Converts 'bad' characters in a string to underscores so they can be used as Ansible groups
190 #> ForemanInventory.to_safe("foo-bar baz")
191 'foo_barbaz'
192 '''
193 regex = r"[^A-Za-z0-9_]"
194 return re.sub(regex, "
", word.replace(" ", ""))
195
196 def _populate(self):
197
198 for host in self._get_hosts():
199
200 if host.get('name'):
201 self.inventory.add_host(host['name'])
202
203 # create directly mapped groups
204 group_name = host.get('hostgroup_title', host.get('hostgroup_name'))
205 if group_name:
206 group_name = self.to_safe('%s%s' % (self.get_option('group_prefix'), group_name.lower()))
207 self.inventory.add_group(group_name)
208 self.inventory.add_child(group_name, host['name'])
209
210 # set host vars from host info
211 try:
212 for k, v in host.items():
213 if k not in ('name', 'hostgroup_title', 'hostgroup_name'):
214 try:
215 self.inventory.set_variable(host['name'], self.get_option('vars_prefix') + k, v)
216 except ValueError as e:
217 self.display.warning("Could not set host info hostvar for %s, skipping %s: %s" % (host, k, to_native(e)))
218 except ValueError as e:
219 self.display.warning("Could not get host info for %s, skipping: %s" % (host['name'], to_native(e)))
220
221 # set host vars from params
222 if self.get_option('want_params'):
223 for p in self._get_all_params_by_id(host['id']):
224 try:
225 self.inventory.set_variable(host['name'], p['name'], p['value'])
226 except ValueError as e:
227 self.display.warning("Could not set parameter hostvar for %s, skipping %s: %s" % (host, p['name'], to_native(p['value'])))
228
229 # set host vars from facts
230 if self.get_option('want_facts'):
231 self.inventory.set_variable(host['name'], 'ansible_facts', self._get_facts(host))
232
233 def parse(self, inventory, loader, path, cache=True):
234
235 super(InventoryModule, self).parse(inventory, loader, path)
236
237 # read config from file, this sets 'options'
238 self._read_config_data(path)
239
240 # get connection host
241 self.foreman_url = self.get_option('url')
242 self.cache_key = self.get_cache_key(path)
243 self.use_cache = cache and self.get_option('cache')
244
245 # actually populate inventory
246 self._populate()

<function_results>1 # CLAUDE.md for Ansible
2
3 ## Project Overview
4 Ansible is an open-source IT automation tool that simplifies configuration management, application deployment, cloud provisioning, ad-hoc task execution, and multinode orchestration. It uses an agentless architecture leveraging SSH for secure, parallel execution across multiple machines. Key components include:
5
6 - 
Playbooks
: YAML files defining automation tasks
7 - 
Modules
: Reusable code units for specific operations (e.g., file management, package installation)
8 - 
Inventory
: Lists of managed hosts and their groupings
9 - 
Roles
: Reusable collections of tasks, variables, and handlers
10 - 
Plugins
: Extend functionality (connection, lookup, callback, etc.)
11
12 Ansible emphasizes simplicity, security, and human-readable automation without requiring custom agents or additional ports.
13
14 ## Key Commands
15 - 
Setup Development Environment
: 
source hacking/env-setup
 (sets PYTHONPATH and PATH for local development)
16 - 
Install Dependencies
: 
pip install -r requirements.txt
 (installs core dependencies: jinja2, PyYAML, paramiko, cryptography)
17 - 
Run Playbook
: 
ansible-playbook playbook.yml -i inventory.ini

18 - 
Ad-hoc Commands
: 
ansible all -i inventory.ini -m ping

19 - 
Test Suite
:
20 - Unit tests: 
ansible-test units

21 - Sanity tests: 
ansible-test sanity

22 - Integration tests: 
ansible-test integration

23 - 
Build
: 
make sdist
 (source distribution) or 
python setup.py build

24 - 
Linting
: 
make pep8
 (flake8) or 
make pyflakes

25 - 
Documentation
: 
make docs
 (rebuilds manpages)
26
27 ## Code Style and Conventions
28 - 
Python Standards
: PEP8 compliant with exceptions:
29 - Max line length: 160 characters
30 - Ignore E402: module-level imports not at top of file
31 - 
File Organization
:
32 - Core library: 
lib/ansible/

33 - Modules: 
lib/ansible/modules/

34 - Plugins: 
lib/ansible/plugins/

35 - Executables: 
bin/

36 - Tests: 
test/
 (sanity, units, integration)
37 - 
Documentation
: RST format for docs, inline comments for code
38 - 
Commit Messages
: Descriptive, following conventional commits where possible
39 - 
Module Development
: Follow checklist in 
MODULE_GUIDELINES.md
, support check_mode, idempotency
40
41 ## Architectural Notes
42 - 
Agentless Design
: Uses SSH/paramiko for connections, no daemons required
43 - 
Modular Architecture
: Core engine in Python, modules can be written in any language
44 - 
Templating
: Jinja2 for variable substitution and logic in playbooks
45 - 
Configuration
: 
ansible.cfg
 for global settings, environment variables override
46 - 
Execution Model
: Parallel task execution, facts gathering, handler system for state changes
47 - 
Security
: Vault for secrets encryption, privilege escalation support
48 - 
Extensibility
: Plugin system for custom connections, lookups, callbacks, and more
49
50 ## Dependencies
51 Core requirements (from requirements.txt):
52 - jinja2: Templating engine
53 - PyYAML: YAML parsing
54 - paramiko: SSH client
55 - cryptography: Encryption support
56
57 Testing dependencies in 
test/runner/requirements/
:
58 - ansible-test framework for comprehensive testing
59 - pytest for unit tests
60 - Various linters and checkers
61
62 ## Rules for Agent
63 - 
Testing
: Always run 
ansible-test sanity
 after code changes to catch issues early
64 - 
Backward Compatibility
: Preserve existing behavior; deprecations require changelog entries
65 - 
Security
: Avoid introducing vulnerabilities; use safe defaults
66 - 
Performance
: Maintain parallel execution capabilities; avoid blocking operations
67 - 
Code Quality
: Follow PEP8 with specified exceptions; use descriptive variable names
68 - 
Documentation
: Update docs for API changes; provide examples for new features
69 - 
Module Standards
: Ensure modules support check_mode, handle errors gracefully, and are idempotent
70 - 
Commit Hygiene
: Use 
make clean
 before builds; avoid committing generated files

Ansible Inventory Plugins Analysis — Docker Swarm Dynamic Inventory Gap

This document summarises how inventory plugins are structured in the Ansible
codebase (
lib/ansible/plugins/inventory/
), how the existing dynamic inventory
plugins integrate with external systems and APIs, and what is missing for a
first-class 
Docker Swarm dynamic inventory
 source. It also proposes an
approach (and concrete file layout) for implementing such a plugin in a way
that is consistent with the rest of the repository.

1. Current inventory plugin structure

All inventory plugins live under 
lib/ansible/plugins/inventory/
. The package
already ships 28 plugins (static file formats: 
ini
, 
yaml
, 
toml
,

host_list
, 
script
; dynamic API-backed plugins: 
aws_ec2
, 
aws_rds
,

azure_rm
, 
gcp_compute
, 
k8s
, 
openshift
, 
foreman
, 
openstack
,

linode
, 
hcloud
, 
netbox
, 
scaleway
, 
tower
, 
vmware_vm_inventory
,

virtualbox
, 
vultr
, 
online
, 
nmap
, ...).

The shared base classes and mixins are defined in

lib/ansible/plugins/inventory/__init__.py
:

BaseInventoryPlugin(AnsiblePlugin)
 — the common parent for every
inventory plugin. Responsibilities:

Holds 
self.inventory
 (an 
InventoryData
 handle), 
self.loader
,

self.templar
, 
self.display
, 
self.cache
 and 
self._options
.

Defines the two methods every plugin overrides:

parse(inventory, loader, path, cache=True)
 — entry point invoked by
the inventory manager. Subclasses first call 
super().parse(...)

and then populate 
self.inventory
 with hosts/groups/variables.

verify_file(path)
 — quick check that the plugin is willing to
consume the given path (usually a filename suffix check in addition
to the base accessibility check).

Provides helpers:

_read_config_data(path)
 — loads the YAML config, validates the

plugin:
 token matches 
self.NAME
, then calls 
set_options(direct=...)

and wires up 
self.cache
 when a cache plugin is requested.

_populate_host_vars(hosts, variables, group, port)
 — convenience
to add hosts and copy variables.

_expand_hostpattern
 / 
expand_hostname_range
 — range expansion for
file-based inventories.

BaseFileInventoryPlugin
 — minor subclass used only by the file
parsers (
ini
, 
yaml
, 
toml
, 
script
, ...).

Cacheable
 — mixin providing 
_cache
, 
get_cache_key(path)
,

_get_cache_prefix(path)
 and 
clear_cache()
. Dynamic plugins combine
this with 
self.cache = InventoryFileCacheModule(...)
 created inside

_set_cache_options()
 so remote API responses can be persisted to
disk via one of the 
cache
 plugins.

Constructable
 — mixin that implements the three knobs documented
in 
lib/ansible/plugins/doc_fragments/constructed.py
:

_compose(template, variables)
 / 
_set_composite_vars(compose, ...)
 —
evaluates the 
compose:
 dict to create Jinja2-derived host variables
(e.g. 
ansible_host: private_ip_address
).

_add_host_to_composed_groups(groups, ...)
 — turns the 
groups:

dict of Jinja2 conditionals into dynamic group memberships.

_add_host_to_keyed_groups(keys, ...)
 — turns the 
keyed_groups:

list into auto-generated groups like 
tag_Environment_dev
 or

aws_region_us_east_1
, running each name through

to_safe_group_name()
 (which underscores non-
[A-Za-z0-9_]
 chars).

A dynamic plugin therefore almost always has the signature:
python
class
 InventoryModule
(
BaseInventoryPlugin, Constructable, Cacheable
)
:
    NAME = 
'my_plugin'
    
def
 verify_file
(
self
, path
)
: ...
    
def
 parse
(
self
, inventory, loader, path, cache=
True
)
: ...
The plugin is only activated if its 
NAME
 is listed in 
INVENTORY_ENABLED

(
lib/ansible/config/base.yml
 defaults to

['host_list', 'script', 'auto', 'yaml', 'ini', 'toml']
). The 
auto
 plugin
(
inventory/auto.py
) is the usual mechanism for loading a plugin by name:
any YAML file that contains 
plugin: <name>
 will be routed by 
auto
 to
the matching plugin via 
inventory_loader.get(plugin_name)
.

2. How dynamic inventory plugins are implemented

Looking at 
aws_ec2.py
, 
linode.py
, 
hcloud.py
, 
k8s.py
, 
foreman.py
,

virtualbox.py
 and 
nmap.py
 the pattern is consistent:

2.1 Documentation block

Each file starts with a top-level 
DOCUMENTATION
 YAML string describing:

name
, 
plugin_type: inventory
, 
short_description
, 
description

requirements:
 (third-party Python libs such as 
boto3
, 
linode_api4
,

openshift
, 
hcloud
, 
requests
, ...)

extends_documentation_fragment:
 — pulls in reusable option blocks such
as 
constructed
 and 
inventory_cache
 so the plugin inherits the

strict
, 
compose
, 
groups
, 
keyed_groups
, 
cache*
 options without
redefining them.

options:
 — each option has a description, 
type
, 
default
,

required
, and optional 
env:
 / 
ini:
 fallbacks. The options are
subsequently retrieved with 
self.get_option('name')
.

2.2 Verifying the inventory source

verify_file
 restricts the plugin to files ending in a conventional suffix:
python
if
 path.endswith
(
(
'aws_ec2.yml'
, 
'aws_ec2.yaml'
)
)
:
    
return
 
True
This is what prevents the 
auto
 plugin from trying to load a random YAML
file with an unrelated 
plugin:
 key.

2.3 Dependency import pattern

Third-party SDKs are imported inside a 
try/except ImportError
 block at
module-load time so that a missing dep fails with a clear Ansible error
rather than a Python traceback, e.g.:
python
try
:
    
import
 boto3
    
import
 botocore
except
 ImportError:
    
raise
 AnsibleError
(
'The ec2 dynamic inventory plugin requires boto3 a
nd botocore.'
)
k8s.py
 uses a slightly softer pattern (a 
HAS_K8S_MODULE_HELPER
 flag
plus a captured 
k8s_import_exception
) so the plugin module itself can
still be imported for introspection; the check then fires inside

setup()
.

2.4 Authentication

Credentials are always resolved in this order:

Options from the YAML config (
self.get_option('access_token')
,

boto_profile
, 
url
/
user
/
password
, 
token
, kubeconfig fields,
...).

Environment variables declared via 
env:
 on the option
(Ansible's config layer applies these automatically, e.g.

LINODE_ACCESS_TOKEN
, 
HCLOUD_TOKEN
, 
AWS_PROFILE
, 
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID
).

SDK-native configuration (e.g. 
botocore.session.get_session()
 in

aws_ec2._set_credentials
, kubeconfig discovery in 
k8s
).

If none of these produce usable credentials, the plugin raises

AnsibleError("Insufficient ... credentials found...")
 before any API
call is attempted.

2.5 Parse / query / populate flow

Every dynamic plugin implements roughly this sequence in 
parse()
:
python
def
 parse
(
self
, inventory, loader, path, cache=
True
)
:
    
super
(
)
.parse
(
inventory, loader, path
)
    
self
._read_config_data
(
path
)
          
# loads YAML -> options
    
self
._set_credentials
(
)
                
# build/validate clients
    cache_key = 
self
.get_cache_key
(
path
)
    
if
 cache:
        cache = 
self
.get_option
(
'cache'
)
    results = 
None
    
if
 cache:
        
try
:
            results = 
self
.cache.get
(
cache_key
)
        
except
 KeyError:
            need_update = 
True
    
if
 results 
is
 
None
:
        results = 
self
._query
(
...
)
         
# hit the remote API
    
self
._populate
(
results
)
                
# add_host / set_variable / groups
    
if
 need_update 
or
 
(
not
 cache 
and
 
self
.get_option
(
'cache'
)
)
:
        
self
.cache.
set
(
cache_key, results
)
The query/populate split is important: caching persists the raw API
response keyed by plugin + config-path hash, and population (including
Constructable grouping) runs on every inventory refresh.

2.6 Error handling

Two conventions stand out:

Wrap every remote call with the SDK's base exception and re-raise as

AnsibleError
 / 
AnsibleParserError
 so the CLI prints an Ansible
message, not a raw traceback. Examples: 
botocore.exceptions.ClientError

in 
aws_ec2._get_instances_by_region
, 
LinodeApiError
 in

linode._get_instances_inventory
, 
DynamicApiError
 in

k8s.get_*_for_namespace
 via 
format_dynamic_api_exc
.

Provide a 
strict_permissions
 / 
strict
 option so that transient
problems (HTTP 403 on a single region, a composed variable failing to
render) can be downgraded to a silent skip instead of aborting the
whole inventory build.

3. How grouping and host variables are handled

3.1 Adding hosts and variables

Inside 
parse()
 plugins interact with 
self.inventory
 (an

InventoryData
 instance) through a small, consistent API:

self.inventory.add_group(name)

self.inventory.add_host(hostname, group=None, port=None)

self.inventory.add_child(group_name, child)

self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, key, value)

linode.py
, 
hcloud.py
 and 
foreman.py
 show the minimal form: one
group-per-property (region/type/image/hostgroup), one 
add_host
 per
instance, then a loop setting hostvars from the raw object/dict.

aws_ec2.py
 shows the richer form (
_add_hosts
):
python
self
.inventory.add_host
(
hostname, group=group
)
host = camel_dict_to_snake_dict
(
host, ignore_list=
[
'Tags'
]
)
host
[
'tags'
]
 = boto3_tag_list_to_ansible_dict
(
host.get
(
'tags'
, 
[
]
)
)
for
 k, v 
in
 host.items
(
)
:
    
self
.inventory.set_variable
(
hostname, k, v
)
strict = 
self
.get_option
(
'strict'
)
self
._set_composite_vars
(
self
.get_option
(
'compose'
)
, host, hostname, strict=strict
)
self
._add_host_to_composed_groups
(
self
.get_option
(
'groups'
)
, host, hostname, strict=strict
)
self
._add_host_to_keyed_groups
(
self
.get_option
(
'keyed_groups'
)
, host, hostname, strict=strict
)
This is the canonical pattern: normalise the API payload into a flat
variable dict, copy it to the host, then delegate to 
Constructable
 for
user-defined composition.

3.2 Grouping strategies

Three distinct flavours appear:

Hard-coded, property-driven groups
 — the plugin creates groups
for well-known facets (
location_nbg1
, 
image_ubuntu
,

server_type_cx11
 in 
hcloud
; per-namespace groups like

namespace_default
, 
namespace_default_pods
, 
namespace_default_services

in 
k8s
; 
aws_ec2
 implicitly creates a single catch-all 
aws_ec2

group via 
_query
 returning 
{'aws_ec2': instances}
 and 
_populate

doing 
add_child('all', group)
).

Label/tag-driven groups
 — iteration over 
metadata.labels
 in

k8s
 produces 
label_<key>_<value>
 groups per pod/service.

User-driven groups via 
Constructable
 — 
keyed_groups
,

groups
, 
compose
 applied uniformly after the plugin has populated
the intrinsic grouping.

Group names are passed through 
to_safe_group_name
 so unusual
characters (spaces, colons, dots from tag values) don't break Ansible's
pattern language.

3.3 Exposing host variables

Plugins expose:

Connection hints
 — typically 
ansible_host
 (via 
compose
 or
explicit 
set_variable
), sometimes 
ansible_connection
 and transport
specifics (
k8s.py
 sets 
ansible_kubectl_pod/container/namespace
).

Raw API attributes
 — either the whole object (
linode
:

instance._raw_json
) or a curated set (
hcloud
: ipv4/ipv6/datacenter/
location/image). 
aws_ec2
 runs the boto3 dict through

camel_dict_to_snake_dict
 and converts tags into a dict so users can
write 
tags.Environment
.

Plugin-specific prefixes
 — 
foreman
 prepends 
foreman_
 (via the

vars_prefix
 option) to each variable it injects to avoid collisions.

4. Dependency handling and connection patterns

Optional imports
 live at module top level inside 
try/except ImportError:
; either the plugin raises a descriptive 
AnsibleError

immediately (
linode
, 
hcloud
, 
aws_ec2
, 
foreman
) or it captures
the error and defers failure to 
parse()
 (
k8s
).

Version pinning
 is encoded in 
requirements:
 in the

DOCUMENTATION
 block and can be enforced at runtime with

distutils.version.LooseVersion
 (
foreman.py
 refuses 
requests < 1.1.0
).

Connection objects
 are usually owned by the plugin instance
(
self.client = LinodeClient(...)
, 
self.session = requests.session()
).
The client is created lazily inside a helper (
_build_client
,

_configure_hcloud_client
, 
_get_session
) so the documentation
options and environment variables can be resolved first.

Transport options
 frequently come from a reusable doc fragment.
For Docker, 
lib/ansible/plugins/doc_fragments/docker.py
 already
declares the canonical option names: 
docker_host
, 
tls
,

tls_verify
, 
tls_hostname
, 
cacert_path
, 
cert_path
, 
key_path
,

ssl_version
, 
api_version
, 
timeout
, 
debug
, together with their

DOCKER_*
 environment variable fallbacks.

Docker SDK glue
 for modules already lives in

lib/ansible/module_utils/docker/common.py
 (
AnsibleDockerClient
,

DOCKER_COMMON_ARGS
, 
MIN_DOCKER_VERSION
, TLS/SSL handling) and

lib/ansible/module_utils/docker/swarm.py

(
AnsibleDockerSwarmClient
 with helpers such as

get_all_nodes_inspect
, 
get_all_nodes_list
, 
get_node_inspect
,

check_if_swarm_manager
).

5. Missing functionality for Docker Swarm support

5.1 What exists today

lib/ansible/modules/cloud/docker/docker_swarm.py
,

docker_swarm_service.py
, 
docker_swarm_facts.py
,

docker_node.py
, 
docker_node_facts.py
 — 
action modules
 that run

on
 a Swarm manager after the inventory has been resolved.

lib/ansible/module_utils/docker/swarm.py
 — manager-side helpers
(
AnsibleDockerSwarmClient
) that can enumerate nodes, services and
tasks.

lib/ansible/plugins/doc_fragments/docker.py
 — reusable docker
connection option fragment.

Community-contributed connection plugins (
docker
) for running tasks
inside running containers.

5.2 What does 
not
 exist

There is 
no
 entry in 
lib/ansible/plugins/inventory/
 that targets
Docker or Docker Swarm. A 
ls
 of that directory (28 files) shows
nothing matching 
docker*
. Consequently:

Users cannot point Ansible at a Swarm manager URL/socket and have the
manager and worker 
nodes
 materialise as inventory hosts with
groups for role (
manager
/
worker
), availability
(
active
/
pause
/
drain
), engine version, datacenter label, custom
node labels, leader status, etc.

Swarm 
services
, 
tasks
 and 
stacks
 cannot be exposed as
hosts/groups either, even though 
AnsibleDockerSwarmClient
 already
knows how to enumerate them via the Docker API.

Today, operators must fall back to a 
static inventory
 (hand-rolled
INI/YAML listing each node hostname), a home-grown 
script

inventory
 that shells out to 
docker node ls --format ...
, or the

community.docker
 Galaxy collection (not shipped in this tree).

Because there is no plugin, none of the Constructable features
(
compose
, 
groups
, 
keyed_groups
) nor the inventory cache
(
cache
, 
cache_plugin
, 
cache_timeout
) are available for Swarm
clusters out of the box.

There are no unit tests under

test/units/plugins/inventory/test_docker_swarm.py
, no integration
tests, and no sample config under 
examples/
.

5.3 User-visible limitations

No automatic discovery when nodes are added/drained/removed — the
inventory is only as fresh as the operator's last manual edit.

No dynamic grouping by Swarm label (
engine.labels.*
,

node.labels.*
), which is the main mechanism Swarm users rely on to
target workloads.

Rolling playbooks across 
manager
 vs 
worker
 nodes requires
duplicating host names in the static inventory.

No uniform authentication/TLS path — every user re-implements
environment variable handling for 
DOCKER_HOST
, 
DOCKER_TLS_VERIFY
,

DOCKER_CERT_PATH
, etc.

No host-variable exposure of node metadata (IDs, addresses, labels,
engine version, availability) for use in templates.

6. Files that would need to be added for a Docker Swarm plugin

Following the same conventions as the existing plugins, a minimal
patch would introduce:

lib/ansible/plugins/inventory/docker_swarm.py
 — the plugin
itself. Class signature:

python
   
class
 InventoryModule
(
BaseInventoryPlugin, Constructable, Cacheable
)
:
       NAME = 
'docker_swarm'
   
It would:

declare 
DOCUMENTATION
 with

extends_documentation_fragment: [docker, constructed, inventory_cache]

plus Swarm-specific options (
verbose_output
, 
include_host_uri
,

include_host_uri_port
, 
use_ssh_host
);

implement 
verify_file
 to accept 
*docker_swarm.yml
 /

*docker_swarm.yaml
;

implement 
parse
 following the query/populate/cache template from

aws_ec2.py
.

test/units/plugins/inventory/test_docker_swarm.py
 —

pytest.importorskip('docker')
 plus tests mirroring

test_linode.py
 (option validation, credential detection, node
grouping, hostvar shape).

test/integration/targets/inventory_docker_swarm/
 — optional
integration target with a minimal Swarm bring-up (if CI allows) or
at least a YAML config + mocked responses.

examples/docker_swarm.yml
 (or under

docs/docsite/rst/user_guide/
) — documented sample config.

changelogs/fragments/<nnn>-docker-swarm-inventory.yaml
 — a

minor_changes:
 entry, per the repo's changelog-fragment policy.

(Nothing needs to change in 
INVENTORY_ENABLED
 defaults because the

auto
 plugin will route any 
plugin: docker_swarm
 YAML file to the
new module as soon as the user enables it.)

The plugin should reuse, not re-implement:

lib/ansible/module_utils/docker/common.py
 for the Docker client
(
AnsibleDockerClient
 / the plain 
docker.APIClient
 when running
outside a module context) plus the 
DOCKER_*
 env-var mapping already
in 
DOCKER_COMMON_ARGS
.

lib/ansible/module_utils/docker/swarm.py
 — particularly

AnsibleDockerSwarmClient.get_all_nodes_inspect()
 and

get_all_nodes_list(output='long')
 for enumeration. Because that
class subclasses 
AnsibleDockerClient
 (which in turn subclasses

AnsibleModule
), the inventory plugin should either:
a) refactor the Swarm helpers into a plain-Python mixin/function
module so they can be called without an 
AnsibleModule
 instance,
or
b) instantiate a low-level 
docker.APIClient
 directly from the
plugin and call 
client.nodes()
, 
client.inspect_node(id)
 —
mirroring how 
k8s.py
 uses the 
openshift.dynamic
 client
directly.

lib/ansible/plugins/doc_fragments/docker.py
 to pick up the full set
of connection options for free.

7. Approaches to add the new plugin

7.1 Option A — minimal plugin (recommended first step)

Match the scope of 
linode.py
 / 
hcloud.py
:

Connect to the Swarm manager using options from the 
docker
 doc
fragment (respecting 
DOCKER_HOST
, TLS settings).

Call 
client.nodes()
 to list all nodes.

verify_file
 accepts 
docker_swarm.yml
 / 
docker_swarm.yaml
.

Use each node's 
Description.Hostname
 as 
inventory_hostname
.

Set 
ansible_host
 from 
Status.Addr
 (falling back to

ManagerStatus.Addr
 for manager nodes) or to the node's FQDN.

Create intrinsic groups: 
manager
, 
worker
, 
leader
, one group
per 
Spec.Availability
, per 
Status.State
, per engine version.

Expose hostvars: 
id
, 
hostname
, 
role
, 
availability
,

state
, 
addr
, 
engine_version
, 
labels
, 
platform
,

manager_status
, full raw dict under 
docker_swarm_node
 for
advanced users.

Integrate 
Constructable
 and 
Cacheable
 exactly like

aws_ec2.py
 so users can add 
keyed_groups
, 
compose
, and

cache*
 options without extra plugin code.

7.2 Option B — extended plugin

After A ships, add:

Swarm service and task enumeration (grouping containers by service,
exposing service hostvars) — mirrors 
k8s.py
's pod/service split.

Multi-cluster support via a 
connections:
 list (same pattern as

k8s.py
 
connections:
), so one config file can manage several
Swarm clusters with per-cluster TLS material.

verbose_output
 / 
filters
 / 
include_host_uri
 options so users
can tune the payload.

7.3 Testing & enabling

Add to 
INVENTORY_ENABLED
 via user config
(
ansible.cfg
: 
enable_plugins = auto, docker_swarm, ...
) or rely
on 
auto
 to route the YAML config.

Unit tests: 
ansible-test units --target test/units/plugins/inventory/test_docker_swarm.py
.

Sanity: 
ansible-test sanity --test pep8,validate-modules --include lib/ansible/plugins/inventory/docker_swarm.py
.

Validate locally with:

plaintext
  ansible-inventory -i ./docker_swarm.yml --graph
  ansible-inventory -i ./docker_swarm.yml --list
  
7.4 Key risks to watch

Dependency on 
docker
 Python SDK version
 — use

MIN_DOCKER_VERSION
 from 
module_utils/docker/common.py
 and raise
a clear 
AnsibleError
 on import failure.

AnsibleDockerSwarmClient
 is module-oriented
 — if reused
verbatim it will call 
AnsibleModule.fail_json
. The inventory plugin
must instead catch 
docker.errors.APIError
 and raise

AnsibleError
 / 
AnsibleParserError
, matching the idiom in

k8s.format_dynamic_api_exc
.

Node hostnames vs. connectable addresses
 — Swarm's

Description.Hostname
 is not always resolvable from the Ansible
controller. Expose both and let users select via 
compose: ansible_host: addr
 or a 
use_ssh_host
-style option, as 
hcloud

does with 
connect_with
.

Caching semantics
 — Swarm state is volatile; set a short

cache_timeout
 default in the docs (or leave caching opt-in, as in

aws_ec2
).

8. Summary

Ansible has a well-defined recipe for dynamic inventory:

BaseInventoryPlugin
 + 
Constructable
 + 
Cacheable
, driven by a
documented YAML config, with third-party SDKs imported optionally and
API errors translated into 
AnsibleError
. Every cloud plugin in

lib/ansible/plugins/inventory/
 follows this template.

The repository already contains most of the Docker building blocks
(
module_utils/docker/common.py
, 
module_utils/docker/swarm.py
, the

docker
 doc fragment, Swarm-facing modules) but 
no Docker Swarm
inventory plugin
. The gap is a single plugin file
(
inventory/docker_swarm.py
) plus its tests, changelog fragment and
example config. Adding it as described above would give users dynamic
discovery of Swarm nodes, first-class grouping by role/label/state, and
the full Constructable/cache feature set without any new plumbing in
Ansible core.

DOCUMENTATION

extends_documentation_fragment: [constructed, inventory_cache]
,

,

; all API exceptions wrapped (

,

,

APIException

AnsibleError
/

; a 
strict
 / 
strict_permissions
 option lets transient errors degrade gracefully.

Grouping: intrinsic groups from API facets (region/type/namespace/label) plus user-defined ones via 
Constructable
. Host variables are the raw API payload, often normalized (e.g. 
camel_dict_to_snake_dict
), frequently with a plugin prefix.

Docker building blocks already present: 
lib/ansible/module_utils/docker/common.py
 (
AnsibleDockerClient
, 
DOCKER_COMMON_ARGS
), 
lib/ansible/module_utils/docker/swarm.py
 (
AnsibleDockerSwarmClient
 with 
get_all_nodes_inspect
, 
get_all_nodes_list
, etc.), 
lib/ansible/plugins/doc_fragments/docker.py
, and the 
docker_swarm*
 action modules.

What's missing

ls lib/ansible/plugins/inventory/ | grep docker
 returns nothing — there is 
no
 Docker Swarm inventory plugin. Users must fall back to static inventory, a hand-rolled 
script
 inventory, or an out-of-tree Galaxy collection. Consequently: no dynamic node discovery, no grouping by manager/worker/role/availability/engine-version/labels, no uniform 
DOCKER_*
 env handling, no Constructable or cache integration.

What would need to be added
 (detailed in the file)

lib/ansible/plugins/inventory/docker_swarm.py
 — the plugin itself, following 
aws_ec2.py
/
linode.py
/
hcloud.py
 patterns.

test/units/plugins/inventory/test_docker_swarm.py
 (mirroring 
test_linode.py
).

Optional integration target, example YAML, and a 
changelogs/fragments/*.yaml
 fragment.

Reuse (don't reimplement) the 
docker
 doc fragment and the existing 
module_utils/docker/*
 helpers, with one caveat: 
AnsibleDockerSwarmClient
 ultimately derives from 
AnsibleModule
 and calls 
fail_json
; the inventory plugin should either refactor the Swarm helpers into a plain-Python utility or talk to 
docker.APIClient
 directly (as 
k8s.py
 does with 
openshift.dynamic
) and translate 
docker.errors.APIError
 into 
AnsibleError
.

Suggested rollout
 — start with a minimal plugin (nodes only, intrinsic groups for role/state/availability + 
Constructable
 + 
Cacheable
), then extend to services/tasks/multi-cluster in a follow-up, matching the 
k8s.py
 
connections:
 pattern.

from ansible.errors import AnsibleError, AnsibleParserError
from ansible.module_utils._text import to_native
from ansible.module_utils.six.moves.urllib.parse import urlparse
from ansible.plugins.inventory import BaseInventoryPlugin, Constructable

try:
import docker
from docker.errors import APIError as DockerAPIError
from docker.errors import DockerException, TLSParameterError
from docker.tls import TLSConfig
HAS_DOCKER = True
DOCKER_IMPORT_ERROR = None
except ImportError as exc:
HAS_DOCKER = False
DOCKER_IMPORT_ERROR = to_native(exc)

class InventoryModule(BaseInventoryPlugin, Constructable):
""" Reads Docker Swarm nodes via the Docker Engine API and exposes them as
Ansible inventory hosts. """

NAME = 'docker_swarm'

def verify_file(self, path):
    """ Accept only YAML config files whose basename ends with
        docker_swarm.yml / docker_swarm.yaml. """
    if super(InventoryModule, self).verify_file(path):
        if path.endswith(('docker_swarm.yml', 'docker_swarm.yaml')):
            return True
        self.display.vvv(
            "Skipping: inventory source does not end with 'docker_swarm.yml' or 'docker_swarm.yaml'."
        )
    return False

def _validate_options(self):
    """ Surface invalid or inconsistent configuration before hitting the API. """
    tls = self.get_option('tls')
    validate_certs = self.get_option('validate_certs')
    if tls and validate_certs:
        raise AnsibleParserError(
            "'tls' and 'validate_certs' are mutually exclusive: set 'validate_certs' if you want "
            "server authentication, or 'tls' for an encrypted but unverified connection."
        )

    client_cert = self.get_option('client_cert')
    client_key = self.get_option('client_key')
    if bool(client_cert) != bool(client_key):
        raise AnsibleParserError(
            "'client_cert' and 'client_key' must be provided together when using TLS client authentication."
        )

    timeout = self.get_option('timeout')
    if timeout is not None and timeout <= 0:
        raise AnsibleParserError("'timeout' must be a positive integer, got: %s" % timeout)

def _build_tls_config(self):
    """ Build a docker.tls.TLSConfig from the plugin options, or return None
        when plain (non-TLS) transport is requested. """
    tls = self.get_option('tls')
    validate_certs = self.get_option('validate_certs')
    if not (tls or validate_certs):
        return None

    client_cert = self.get_option('client_cert')
    client_key = self.get_option('client_key')
    ca_cert = self.get_option('ca_cert')
    tls_hostname = self.get_option('tls_hostname')
    ssl_version = self.get_option('ssl_version')

    kwargs = {
        'verify': bool(validate_certs),
        'ssl_version': ssl_version,
    }
    if client_cert and client_key:
        kwargs['client_cert'] = (client_cert, client_key)
    if ca_cert:
        kwargs['ca_cert'] = ca_cert
    if validate_certs and tls_hostname:
        kwargs['assert_hostname'] = tls_hostname

    try:
        return TLSConfig(**kwargs)
    except TLSParameterError as exc:
        raise AnsibleError("Invalid TLS configuration for docker_swarm inventory: %s" % to_native(exc))

def _build_client(self):
    """ Instantiate the low-level Docker APIClient used to talk to the
        Swarm manager. Raises AnsibleError if the SDK is missing or the
        client cannot be created. """
    if not HAS_DOCKER:
        raise AnsibleError(
            "The docker_swarm dynamic inventory plugin requires the Docker SDK for Python "
            "(install with `pip install docker`). Import error: %s" % DOCKER_IMPORT_ERROR
        )

    docker_host = self.get_option('docker_host')
    api_version = self.get_option('api_version')
    timeout = self.get_option('timeout')
    tls_config = self._build_tls_config()

    # Mirror the behavior of ansible's docker modules: a tcp:// URL is
    # implicitly upgraded to https:// when TLS is in use, so that the
    # underlying requests adapter picks the right transport.
    if tls_config is not None and isinstance(docker_host, str) and docker_host.startswith('tcp://'):
        docker_host = 'https://' + docker_host[len('tcp://'):]

    # Reject obviously malformed URLs/paths early.
    parsed = urlparse(docker_host or '')
    if not docker_host or (not parsed.scheme and not docker_host.startswith('/')):
        raise AnsibleParserError(
            "Invalid 'docker_host' value %r: expected a URL like unix://..., tcp://host:port, "
            "https://host:port, or an absolute socket path." % docker_host
        )

    client_kwargs = dict(
        base_url=docker_host,
        version=api_version,
        timeout=timeout,
    )
    if tls_config is not None:
        client_kwargs['tls'] = tls_config

    try:
        return docker.APIClient(**client_kwargs)
    except DockerException as exc:
        raise AnsibleError(
            "Failed to create Docker API client for %s: %s" % (docker_host, to_native(exc))
        )

def _fetch_nodes(self, client):
    """ Return the raw list of node dicts reported by the Swarm manager. """
    try:
        nodes = client.nodes()
    except DockerAPIError as exc:
        raise AnsibleError(
            "Docker API error while listing Swarm nodes. This usually means the target host "
            "is not a Swarm manager or the API version is incompatible. Details: %s" % to_native(exc)
        )
    except DockerException as exc:
        raise AnsibleError(
            "Could not connect to the Docker daemon at %s: %s"
            % (self.get_option('docker_host'), to_native(exc))
        )
    except Exception as exc:
        # Network errors from the underlying requests library surface as
        # assorted exception types; wrap them into a single clear message.
        raise AnsibleError(
            "Unexpected error while querying Swarm nodes from %s: %s"
            % (self.get_option('docker_host'), to_native(exc))
        )

    if not isinstance(nodes, list):
        raise AnsibleError(
            "Unexpected response from the Docker API when listing Swarm nodes: %r" % (nodes,)
        )
    return nodes

def _populate(self, nodes):
    """ Translate the Swarm node payload into Ansible inventory state. """
    # Make sure the intrinsic groups always exist so playbooks can
    # reference them unconditionally, even on an empty cluster.
    for base_group in ('manager', 'worker', 'leader'):
        self.inventory.add_group(base_group)

    verbose_output = self.get_option('verbose_output')
    strict = self.get_option('strict')
    compose = self.get_option('compose')
    groups = self.get_option('groups')
    keyed_groups = self.get_option('keyed_groups')

    for node in nodes:
        if not isinstance(node, dict):
            self.display.warning("Skipping malformed Swarm node entry: %r" % (node,))
            continue

        description = node.get('Description') or {}
        spec = node.get('Spec') or {}
        status = node.get('Status') or {}
        manager_status = node.get('ManagerStatus') or {}
        engine = description.get('Engine') or {}
        platform = description.get('Platform') or {}

        hostname = description.get('Hostname') or node.get('ID')
        if not hostname:
            self.display.warning("Skipping Swarm node without a Hostname or ID: %r" % (node,))
            continue

        role = spec.get('Role') or 'worker'
        # Guard against unexpected roles so we never mis-route a node.
        group_name = role if role in ('manager', 'worker') else 'worker'
        self.inventory.add_host(hostname, group=group_name)

        is_leader = bool(manager_status.get('Leader')) if manager_status else False
        if is_leader:
            self.inventory.add_child('leader', hostname)

        # Prefer the Swarm node's advertised address; fall back to the
        # manager reach address. Either one makes a better ansible_host
        # than the Swarm 'Hostname' (which is only guaranteed to be
        # resolvable from inside the cluster).
        addr = status.get('Addr') or manager_status.get('Addr')

        node_vars = {
            'docker_swarm_id': node.get('ID'),
            'docker_swarm_hostname': hostname,
            'docker_swarm_role': role,
            'docker_swarm_availability': spec.get('Availability'),
            'docker_swarm_state': status.get('State'),
            'docker_swarm_addr': addr,
            'docker_swarm_engine_version': engine.get('EngineVersion'),
            'docker_swarm_labels': spec.get('Labels') or {},
            'docker_swarm_platform_os': platform.get('OS'),
            'docker_swarm_platform_architecture': platform.get('Architecture'),
            'docker_swarm_manager_reachability': manager_status.get('Reachability') if manager_status else None,
            'docker_swarm_manager_leader': is_leader,
        }
        if verbose_output:
            node_vars['docker_swarm_node'] = node

        if addr:
            # Only set ansible_host when we actually have a usable address;
            # otherwise let Ansible use the inventory_hostname verbatim.
            self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'ansible_host', addr)

        for var_name, var_value in node_vars.items():
            if var_value is None:
                continue
            self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, var_name, var_value)

        # Apply Constructable features against the curated var dict so
        # users can build composite vars and custom groups from stable
        # keys regardless of verbose_output.
        self._set_composite_vars(compose, node_vars, hostname, strict=strict)
        self._add_host_to_composed_groups(groups, node_vars, hostname, strict=strict)
        self._add_host_to_keyed_groups(keyed_groups, node_vars, hostname, strict=strict)

def parse(self, inventory, loader, path, cache=True):
    super(InventoryModule, self).parse(inventory, loader, path)

    # Load and validate the YAML config: this also wires up get_option().
    self._read_config_data(path)
    self._validate_options()

    client = self._build_client()
    nodes = self._fetch_nodes(client)
    self._populate(nodes)

139

145 HAS_DOCKER = True
146 DOCKER_IMPORT_ERROR = None
147 except ImportError as exc:
148 HAS_DOCKER = False

152 class InventoryModule(BaseInventoryPlugin, Constructable):
153 """ Reads Docker Swarm nodes via the Docker Engine API and exposes them as
154 Ansible inventory hosts. """
155
156 NAME = 'docker_swarm'
157
158 def verify_file(self, path):
159 """ Accept only YAML config files whose basename ends with
160 docker_swarm.yml / docker_swarm.yaml. """
161 if super(InventoryModule, self).verify_file(path):
162 if path.endswith(('docker_swarm.yml', 'docker_swarm.yaml')):
163 return True
164 self.display.vvv(
165 "Skipping: inventory source does not end with 'docker_swarm.yml' or 'docker_swarm.yaml'."
166 )
167 return False
168
169 def _validate_options(self):
170 """ Surface invalid or inconsistent configuration before hitting the API. """
171 tls = self.get_option('tls')
172 validate_certs = self.get_option('validate_certs')
173 if tls and validate_certs:
174 raise AnsibleParserError(
175 "'tls' and 'validate_certs' are mutually exclusive: set 'validate_certs' if you want "
176 "server authentication, or 'tls' for an encrypted but unverified connection."
177 )
178
179 client_cert = self.get_option('client_cert')
180 client_key = self.get_option('client_key')
181 if bool(client_cert) != bool(client_key):
182 raise AnsibleParserError(
183 "'client_cert' and 'client_key' must be provided together when using TLS client authentication."
184 )
185
186 timeout = self.get_option('timeout')
187 if timeout is not None and timeout <= 0:
188 raise AnsibleParserError("'timeout' must be a positive integer, got: %s" % timeout)
189
190 def _build_tls_config(self):
191 """ Build a docker.tls.TLSConfig from the plugin options, or return None
192 when plain (non-TLS) transport is requested. """
193 tls = self.get_option('tls')
194 validate_certs = self.get_option('validate_certs')
195 if not (tls or validate_certs):
196 return None
197
198 client_cert = self.get_option('client_cert')
199 client_key = self.get_option('client_key')
200 ca_cert = self.get_option('ca_cert')
201 tls_hostname = self.get_option('tls_hostname')
202 ssl_version = self.get_option('ssl_version')
203
204 kwargs = {
205 'verify': bool(validate_certs),
206 'ssl_version': ssl_version,
207 }
208 if client_cert and client_key:
209 kwargs['client_cert'] = (client_cert, client_key)
210 if ca_cert:
211 kwargs['ca_cert'] = ca_cert
212 if validate_certs and tls_hostname:
213 kwargs['assert_hostname'] = tls_hostname
214
215 try:
216 return TLSConfig(**kwargs)
217 except TLSParameterError as exc:
218 raise AnsibleError("Invalid TLS configuration for docker_swarm inventory: %s" % to_native(exc))
219
220 def _build_client(self):
221 """ Instantiate the low-level Docker APIClient used to talk to the
222 Swarm manager. Raises AnsibleError if the SDK is missing or the
223 client cannot be created. """
224 if not HAS_DOCKER:
225 raise AnsibleError(
226 "The docker_swarm dynamic inventory plugin requires the Docker SDK for Python "
227 "(install with 
pip install docker
). Import error: %s" % DOCKER_IMPORT_ERROR
228 )
229
230 docker_host = self.get_option('docker_host')
231 api_version = self.get_option('api_version')
232 timeout = self.get_option('timeout')
233 tls_config = self._build_tls_config()
234
235 # Mirror the behavior of ansible's docker modules: a tcp:// URL is
236 # implicitly upgraded to https:// when TLS is in use, so that the
237 # underlying requests adapter picks the right transport.
238 if tls_config is not None and isinstance(docker_host, str) and docker_host.startswith('tcp://'):
239 docker_host = 'https://' + docker_host[len('tcp://'):]
240
241 # Reject obviously malformed URLs/paths early: we expect either a
242 # scheme-qualified URL (unix://, tcp://, http://, https://) or an
243 # absolute filesystem path to a Unix socket.
244 if not docker_host or (
245 '://' not in docker_host and not docker_host.startswith('/')
246 ):
247 raise AnsibleParserError(
248 "Invalid 'docker_host' value %r: expected a URL like unix://..., tcp://host:port, "
249 "
https://host:port
, or an absolute socket path." % docker_host
250 )
251
252 client_kwargs = dict(
253 base_url=docker_host,
254 version=api_version,
255 timeout=timeout,
256 )
257 if tls_config is not None:
258 client_kwargs['tls'] = tls_config
259
260 try:
261 return docker.APIClient(**client_kwargs)
262 except DockerException as exc:
263 raise AnsibleError(
264 "Failed to create Docker API client for %s: %s" % (docker_host, to_native(exc))
265 )
266
267 def _fetch_nodes(self, client):
268 """ Return the raw list of node dicts reported by the Swarm manager. """
269 try:
270 nodes = client.nodes()
271 except DockerAPIError as exc:
272 raise AnsibleError(
273 "Docker API error while listing Swarm nodes. This usually means the target host "
274 "is not a Swarm manager or the API version is incompatible. Details: %s" % to_native(exc)
275 )
276 except DockerException as exc:
277 raise AnsibleError(
278 "Could not connect to the Docker daemon at %s: %s"
279 % (self.get_option('docker_host'), to_native(exc))
280 )
281 except Exception as exc:
282 # Network errors from the underlying requests library surface as
283 # assorted exception types; wrap them into a single clear message.
284 raise AnsibleError(
285 "Unexpected error while querying Swarm nodes from %s: %s"
286 % (self.get_option('docker_host'), to_native(exc))
287 )
288
289 if not isinstance(nodes, list):
290 raise AnsibleError(
291 "Unexpected response from the Docker API when listing Swarm nodes: %r" % (nodes,)
292 )
293 return nodes
294
295 def _populate(self, nodes):
296 """ Translate the Swarm node payload into Ansible inventory state. """
297 # Make sure the intrinsic groups always exist so playbooks can
298 # reference them unconditionally, even on an empty cluster.
299 for base_group in ('manager', 'worker', 'leader'):
300 self.inventory.add_group(base_group)
301
302 verbose_output = self.get_option('verbose_output')
303 strict = self.get_option('strict')
304 compose = self.get_option('compose')
305 groups = self.get_option('groups')
306 keyed_groups = self.get_option('keyed_groups')
307
308 for node in nodes:
309 if not isinstance(node, dict):
310 self.display.warning("Skipping malformed Swarm node entry: %r" % (node,))
311 continue
312
313 description = node.get('Description') or {}
314 spec = node.get('Spec') or {}
315 status = node.get('Status') or {}
316 manager_status = node.get('ManagerStatus') or {}
317 engine = description.get('Engine') or {}
318 platform = description.get('Platform') or {}
319
320 hostname = description.get('Hostname') or node.get('ID')
321 if not hostname:
322 self.display.warning("Skipping Swarm node without a Hostname or ID: %r" % (node,))
323 continue
324
325 role = spec.get('Role') or 'worker'
326 # Guard against unexpected roles so we never mis-route a node.
327 group_name = role if role in ('manager', 'worker') else 'worker'
328 self.inventory.add_host(hostname, group=group_name)
329
330 is_leader = bool(manager_status.get('Leader')) if manager_status else False
331 if is_leader:
332 self.inventory.add_child('leader', hostname)
333
334 # Prefer the Swarm node's advertised address; fall back to the
335 # manager reach address. Either one makes a better ansible_host
336 # than the Swarm 'Hostname' (which is only guaranteed to be
337 # resolvable from inside the cluster).
338 addr = status.get('Addr') or manager_status.get('Addr')
339
340 node_vars = {
341 'docker_swarm_id': node.get('ID'),
342 'docker_swarm_hostname': hostname,
343 'docker_swarm_role': role,
344 'docker_swarm_availability': spec.get('Availability'),
345 'docker_swarm_state': status.get('State'),
346 'docker_swarm_addr': addr,
347 'docker_swarm_engine_version': engine.get('EngineVersion'),
348 'docker_swarm_labels': spec.get('Labels') or {},
349 'docker_swarm_platform_os': platform.get('OS'),
350 'docker_swarm_platform_architecture': platform.get('Architecture'),
351 'docker_swarm_manager_reachability': manager_status.get('Reachability') if manager_status else None,
352 'docker_swarm_manager_leader': is_leader,
353 }
354 if verbose_output:
355 node_vars['docker_swarm_node'] = node
356
357 if addr:
358 # Only set ansible_host when we actually have a usable address;
359 # otherwise let Ansible use the inventory_hostname verbatim.
360 self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'ansible_host', addr)
361
362 for var_name, var_value in node_vars.items():
363 if var_value is None:
364 continue
365 self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, var_name, var_value)
366
367 # Apply Constructable features against the curated var dict so
368 # users can build composite vars and custom groups from stable
369 # keys regardless of verbose_output.
370 self._set_composite_vars(compose, node_vars, hostname, strict=strict)
371 self._add_host_to_composed_groups(groups, node_vars, hostname, strict=strict)
372 self._add_host_to_keyed_groups(keyed_groups, node_vars, hostname, strict=strict)
373
374 def parse(self, inventory, loader, path, cache=True):
375 super(InventoryModule, self).parse(inventory, loader, path)
376
377 # Load and validate the YAML config: this also wires up get_option().
378 self._read_config_data(path)
379 self._validate_options()
380
381 client = self._build_client()
382 nodes = self._fetch_nodes(client)
383 self._populate(nodes)

49 def detect_range(line=None):
50 '''
51 A helper function that checks a given host line to see if it contains
52 a range pattern described in the docstring above.
53
54 Returns True if the given line contains a pattern, else False.
55 '''
56 return '[' in line
57
58
59 def expand_hostname_range(line=None):
60 '''
61 A helper function that expands a given line that contains a pattern
62 specified in top docstring, and returns a list that consists of the
63 expanded version.
64
65 The '[' and ']' characters are used to maintain the pseudo-code
66 appearance. They are replaced in this function with '|' to ease
67 string splitting.
68
69 References: 
https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/user_guide/intro_inventory.html#hosts-and-groups

70 '''
71 all_hosts = []
72 if line:
73 # A hostname such as db[1:6]-node is considered to consists
74 # three parts:
75 # head: 'db'
76 # nrange: [1:6]; range() is a built-in. Can't use the name
77 # tail: '-node'
78
79 # Add support for multiple ranges in a host so:
80 # db[01:10:3]node-[01:10]
81 # - to do this we split off at the first [...] set, getting the list
82 # of hosts and then repeat until none left.
83 # - also add an optional third parameter which contains the step. (Default: 1)
84 # so range can be [01:10:2] -> 01 03 05 07 09
85
86 (head, nrange, tail) = line.replace('[', '|', 1).replace(']', '|', 1).split('|')
87 bounds = nrange.split(":")
88 if len(bounds) != 2 and len(bounds) != 3:
89 raise AnsibleError("host range must be begin:end or begin:end:step")
90 beg = bounds[0]
91 end = bounds[1]
92 if len(bounds) == 2:
93 step = 1
94 else:
95 step = bounds[2]
96 if not beg:
97 beg = "0"
98 if not end:
99 raise AnsibleError("host range must specify end value")
100 if beg[0] == '0' and len(beg) > 1:
101 rlen = len(beg) # range length formatting hint
102 if rlen != len(end):
103 raise AnsibleError("host range must specify equal-length begin and end formats")
104
105 def fill(x):
106 return str(x).zfill(rlen) # range sequence
107
108 else:
109 fill = str
110
111 try:
112 i_beg = string.ascii_letters.index(beg)
113 i_end = string.ascii_letters.index(end)
114 if i_beg > i_end:
115 raise AnsibleError("host range must have begin <= end")
116 seq = list(string.ascii_letters[i_beg:i_end + 1:int(step)])
117 except ValueError: # not an alpha range
118 seq = range(int(beg), int(end) + 1, int(step))
119
120 for rseq in seq:
121 hname = ''.join((head, fill(rseq), tail))
122
123 if detect_range(hname):
124 all_hosts.extend(expand_hostname_range(hname))
125 else:
126 all_hosts.append(hname)
127
128 return all_hosts

145 def parse(self, inventory, loader, path, cache=True):
146 ''' Populates inventory from the given data. Raises an error on any parse failure
147 :arg inventory: a copy of the previously accumulated inventory data,
148 to be updated with any new data this plugin provides.
149 The inventory can be empty if no other source/plugin ran successfully.
150 :arg loader: a reference to the DataLoader, which can read in YAML and JSON files,
151 it also has Vault support to automatically decrypt files.
152 :arg path: the string that represents the 'inventory source',
153 normally a path to a configuration file for this inventory,
154 but it can also be a raw string for this plugin to consume
155 :arg cache: a boolean that indicates if the plugin should use the cache or not
156 you can ignore if this plugin does not implement caching.
157 '''
158
159 self.loader = loader
160 self.inventory = inventory
161 self.templar = Templar(loader=loader)
162
163 def verify_file(self, path):
164 ''' Verify if file is usable by this plugin, base does minimal accessibility check
165 :arg path: a string that was passed as an inventory source,
166 it normally is a path to a config file, but this is not a requirement,
167 it can also be parsed itself as the inventory data to process.
168 So only call this base class if you expect it to be a file.
169 '''
170
171 valid = False
172 b_path = to_bytes(path, errors='surrogate_or_strict')
173 if (os.path.exists(b_path) and os.access(b_path, os.R_OK)):
174 valid = True
175 else:
176 self.display.vvv('Skipping due to inventory source not existing or not being readable by the current user')
177 return valid

180 if not isinstance(variables, Mapping):
181 raise AnsibleParserError("Invalid data from file, expected dictionary and got:\n\n%s" % to_native(variables))
182
183 for host in hosts:
184 self.inventory.add_host(host, group=group, port=port)
185 for k in variables:
186 self.inventory.set_variable(host, k, variables[k])

191 '''
192
193 config = {}
194 try:
195 # avoid loader cache so meta: refresh_inventory can pick up config changes
196 # if we read more than once, fs cache should be good enough
197 config = self.loader.load_from_file(path, cache=False)
198 except Exception as e:
199 raise AnsibleParserError(to_native(e))
200
201 if not config:
202 # no data
203 raise AnsibleParserError("%s is empty" % (to_native(path)))
204 elif config.get('plugin') != self.NAME:
205 # this is not my config file
206 raise AnsibleParserError("Incorrect plugin name in file: %s" % config.get('plugin', 'none found'))
207 elif not isinstance(config, Mapping):
208 # configs are dictionaries
209 raise AnsibleParserError('inventory source has invalid structure, it should be a dictionary, got: %s' % type(config))

214

234 Takes a single host pattern and returns a list of hostnames and an

297 def _compose(self, template, variables):
298 ''' helper method for plugins to compose variables for Ansible based on jinja2 expression and inventory vars'''
299 t = self.templar
300 t.set_available_variables(variables)

303 def _set_composite_vars(self, compose, variables, host, strict=False):
304 ''' loops over compose entries to create vars for hosts '''
305 if compose and isinstance(compose, dict):
306 for varname in compose:
307 try:
308 composite = self._compose(compose[varname], variables)
309 except Exception as e:
310 if strict:
311 raise AnsibleError("Could not set %s for host %s: %s" % (varname, host, to_native(e)))
312 continue
313 self.inventory.set_variable(host, varname, composite)

315 def _add_host_to_composed_groups(self, groups, variables, host, strict=False):
316 ''' helper to create complex groups for plugins based on jinja2 conditionals, hosts that meet the conditional are added to group'''
317 # process each 'group entry'
318 if groups and isinstance(groups, dict):
319 self.templar.set_available_variables(variables)
320 for group_name in groups:
321 conditional = "{%% if %s %%} True {%% else %%} False {%% endif %%}" % groups[group_name]
322 try:
323 result = boolean(self.templar.template(conditional))
324 except Exception as e:
325 if strict:
326 raise AnsibleParserError("Could not add host %s to group %s: %s" % (host, group_name, to_native(e)))
327 continue
328
329 if result:
330 # ensure group exists
331 self.inventory.add_group(group_name)
332 # add host to group
333 self.inventory.add_child(group_name, host)

335 def _add_host_to_keyed_groups(self, keys, variables, host, strict=False):
336 ''' helper to create groups for plugins based on variable values and add the corresponding hosts to it'''
337 if keys and isinstance(keys, list):
338 groups = []
339 for keyed in keys:
340 if keyed and isinstance(keyed, dict):
341
342 try:
343 key = self.
compose(keyed.get('key'), variables)
344 except Exception as e:
345 if strict:
346 raise AnsibleParserError("Could not generate group for host %s from %s entry: %s" % (host, keyed.get('key'), to_native(e)))
347 continue
348
349 if key:
350 prefix = keyed.get('prefix', '')
351 sep = keyed.get('separator', '
')
352
353 if isinstance(key, string_types):
354 groups.append('%s%s%s' % (prefix, sep, key))
355 elif isinstance(key, list):
356 for name in key:
357 groups.append('%s%s%s' % (prefix, sep, name))
358 elif isinstance(key, Mapping):
359 for (gname, gval) in key.items():
360 name = '%s%s%s' % (gname, sep, gval)
361 groups.append('%s%s%s' % (prefix, sep, name))
362 else:
363 raise AnsibleParserError("Invalid group name format, expected a string or a list of them or dictionary, got: %s" % type(key))
364 else:
365 if strict:
366 raise AnsibleParserError("No key or key resulted empty, invalid entry")
367 else:
368 raise AnsibleParserError("Invalid keyed group entry, it must be a dictionary: %s " % keyed)
369
370 # now actually add any groups
371 for group_name in groups:
372 gname = to_safe_group_name(group_name)
373 self.inventory.add_group(gname)
374 self.inventory.add_child(gname, host)

name: docker_swarm

short_description: Ansible dynamic inventory plugin for Docker Swarm nodes.

https://docker-py.readthedocs.io/en/stable/
) >= 2.4.0"

description:
- Reads inventories from Docker Swarm.
- Uses a YAML configuration file that ends with C(docker_swarm.(yml|yaml)).
- Uses the Docker Engine API to query the list of swarm nodes.

and optionally by other attributes such as availability and status.
options:

docker_host:

- To connect to a remote host, provide the TCP connection string
(for example C(tcp://192.0.2.23:2376)).

automatically replace C(tcp) in the URL with C(https).
type: str
default: unix://var/run/docker.sock
env:
- name: DOCKER_HOST
tls:
description:
- Secure the connection to the API by using TLS without verifying
the authenticity of the Docker host server.
type: bool
default: false
env:

description:
- Secure the connection to the API by using TLS and verifying

- Defaults to the latest version of the API supported by the
Docker SDK for Python installed.

description:
- The maximum amount of time in seconds to wait on a response from the API.

default: 60

- name: DOCKER_TIMEOUT
cacert_path:
description:
- Use a CA certificate when performing server verification by
providing the path to a CA certificate file.
type: path
env:
- name: DOCKER_CERT_PATH
cert_path:

import os

from ansible.errors import AnsibleError
from ansible.module_utils._text import to_native
from ansible.module_utils.six.moves.urllib.parse import urlparse
from ansible.plugins.inventory import BaseInventoryPlugin, Constructable, to_safe_group_name
from ansible.utils.display import Display

HAS_DOCKER = False
HAS_DOCKER_ERROR = None

try:
from docker import APIClient
from docker import 
version
 as docker_version
from docker.errors import APIError, DockerException, TLSParameterError
from docker.tls import TLSConfig
HAS_DOCKER = True
except ImportError as exc:
HAS_DOCKER_ERROR = str(exc)

try:
from docker.errors import NotFound # noqa: F401
except ImportError:
pass

display = Display()

MIN_DOCKER_API_VERSION = '1.24'

class InventoryModule(BaseInventoryPlugin, Constructable):

NAME = 'docker_swarm'

def _fail(self, msg):
    raise AnsibleError(msg)

def _get_tls_config(self, **kwargs):
    try:
        tls_config = TLSConfig(**kwargs)
        return tls_config
    except TLSParameterError as exc:
        self._fail("TLS config error: %s" % to_native(exc))

def _get_connect_params(self):
    docker_host = self.get_option('docker_host')
    tls = self.get_option('tls')
    tls_verify = self.get_option('tls_verify')
    tls_hostname = self.get_option('tls_hostname')
    ssl_version = self.get_option('ssl_version')
    cert_path = self.get_option('cert_path')
    key_path = self.get_option('key_path')
    cacert_path = self.get_option('cacert_path')
    api_version = self.get_option('api_version')
    timeout = self.get_option('timeout')

    # Resolve DOCKER_CERT_PATH: when the env is a directory, build
    # individual file paths as the Docker CLI and module_utils do.
    for attr, filename in [('cert_path', 'cert.pem'),
                           ('key_path', 'key.pem'),
                           ('cacert_path', 'ca.pem')]:
        value = locals().get(attr)
        if value and os.path.isdir(value):
            locals()[attr]  # noqa – just to clarify the intent
            if attr == 'cert_path':
                cert_path = os.path.join(value, filename)
            elif attr == 'key_path':
                key_path = os.path.join(value, filename)
            elif attr == 'cacert_path':
                cacert_path = os.path.join(value, filename)

    if tls_verify and tls:
        self._fail("tls and tls_verify are mutually exclusive.")

    if tls or tls_verify:
        docker_host = docker_host.replace('tcp://', 'https://')

    if tls and cert_path and key_path:
        tls_config = self._get_tls_config(client_cert=(cert_path, key_path),
                                          verify=False,
                                          ssl_version=ssl_version)
        return dict(base_url=docker_host, tls=tls_config,
                    version=api_version, timeout=timeout)

    if tls:
        tls_config = self._get_tls_config(verify=False,
                                          ssl_version=ssl_version)
        return dict(base_url=docker_host, tls=tls_config,
                    version=api_version, timeout=timeout)

    if tls_verify and cert_path and key_path:
        if cacert_path:
            tls_config = self._get_tls_config(
                client_cert=(cert_path, key_path),
                ca_cert=cacert_path,
                verify=True,
                assert_hostname=tls_hostname,
                ssl_version=ssl_version)
        else:
            tls_config = self._get_tls_config(
                client_cert=(cert_path, key_path),
                verify=True,
                assert_hostname=tls_hostname,
                ssl_version=ssl_version)
        return dict(base_url=docker_host, tls=tls_config,
                    version=api_version, timeout=timeout)

    if tls_verify and cacert_path:
        tls_config = self._get_tls_config(
            ca_cert=cacert_path,
            verify=True,
            assert_hostname=tls_hostname,
            ssl_version=ssl_version)
        return dict(base_url=docker_host, tls=tls_config,
                    version=api_version, timeout=timeout)

    if tls_verify:
        tls_config = self._get_tls_config(
            verify=True,
            assert_hostname=tls_hostname,
            ssl_version=ssl_version)
        return dict(base_url=docker_host, tls=tls_config,
                    version=api_version, timeout=timeout)

    # No TLS
    return dict(base_url=docker_host,
                version=api_version, timeout=timeout)

def _get_docker_client(self):
    if not HAS_DOCKER:
        self._fail(
            "The docker_swarm dynamic inventory plugin requires the Docker SDK for Python: "
            "https://docker-py.readthedocs.io/en/stable/ (error: %s)" % HAS_DOCKER_ERROR
        )

    connect_params = self._get_connect_params()
    try:
        self.client = APIClient(**connect_params)
    except DockerException as exc:
        self._fail("Error connecting to Docker: %s" % to_native(exc))

def _get_swarm_nodes(self):
    try:
        self.nodes = self.client.nodes()
    except APIError as exc:
        self._fail(
            "Error fetching node list from Docker Swarm. "
            "Are you connecting to a Swarm manager? Error: %s" % to_native(exc)
        )

def _populate(self):
    # Create default role groups
    self.inventory.add_group('manager')
    self.inventory.add_group('worker')
    self.inventory.add_group('leader')

    strict = self.get_option('strict')
    verbose_output = self.get_option('verbose_output')

    for node in self.nodes:
        self._process_node(node, strict, verbose_output)

def _process_node(self, node, strict, verbose_output):
    # Derive the host identity from the node hostname
    hostname = node.get('Description', {}).get('Hostname')
    if not hostname:
        display.warning("Skipping node %s: no hostname available" % node.get('ID', 'unknown'))
        return

    self.inventory.add_host(hostname)

    # --- Determine role and leader status ---
    spec = node.get('Spec', {})
    role = spec.get('Role', 'worker')
    availability = spec.get('Availability', 'active')

    status_info = node.get('Status', {})
    state = status_info.get('State', 'unknown')
    addr = status_info.get('Addr', '')

    manager_status = node.get('ManagerStatus', {})
    leader = manager_status.get('Leader', False)
    manager_addr = manager_status.get('Addr', '')
    reachability = manager_status.get('Reachability', '')

    description = node.get('Description', {})
    engine_version = description.get('Engine', {}).get('EngineVersion', '')
    platform_os = description.get('Platform', {}).get('OS', '')
    platform_arch = description.get('Platform', {}).get('Architecture', '')

    node_labels = spec.get('Labels') or {}
    engine_labels = description.get('Engine', {}).get('Labels') or {}

    # --- Group assignment ---
    # Role-based groups
    role_group = to_safe_group_name(role)
    if role_group not in ('manager', 'worker'):
        self.inventory.add_group(role_group)
    self.inventory.add_child(role_group, hostname)

    # Leader group
    if leader:
        self.inventory.add_child('leader', hostname)

    # --- Host variables ---
    # Set ansible_host so Ansible can reach the node
    node_addr = addr or manager_addr
    if node_addr:
        # Strip the port from manager addresses (e.g. "10.0.0.1:2377")
        if ':' in node_addr:
            parsed = urlparse('//' + node_addr)
            node_addr_host = parsed.hostname or node_addr
        else:
            node_addr_host = node_addr
        self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'ansible_host', node_addr_host)

    self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'docker_swarm_node_id', node.get('ID', ''))
    self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'docker_swarm_node_hostname', hostname)
    self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'docker_swarm_node_role', role)
    self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'docker_swarm_node_availability', availability)
    self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'docker_swarm_node_state', state)
    self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'docker_swarm_node_addr', addr)
    self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'docker_swarm_node_engine_version', engine_version)
    self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'docker_swarm_node_platform_os', platform_os)
    self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'docker_swarm_node_platform_arch', platform_arch)
    self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'docker_swarm_node_labels', node_labels)
    self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'docker_swarm_node_engine_labels', engine_labels)
    self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'docker_swarm_node_leader', leader)
    self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'docker_swarm_node_manager_addr', manager_addr)
    self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'docker_swarm_node_reachability', reachability)

    if verbose_output:
        self.inventory.set_variable(hostname, 'docker_swarm_node_attributes', node)

    # --- Constructable ---
    # Build a flat dict of all host vars for Jinja2 templating
    hostvars = {
        'docker_swarm_node_id': node.get('ID', ''),
        'docker_swarm_node_hostname': hostname,
        'docker_swarm_node_role': role,
        'docker_swarm_node_availability': availability,
        'docker_swarm_node_state': state,
        'docker_swarm_node_addr': addr,
        'docker_swarm_node_engine_version': engine_version,
        'docker_swarm_node_platform_os': platform_os,
        'docker_swarm_node_platform_arch': platform_arch,
        'docker_swarm_node_labels': node_labels,
        'docker_swarm_node_engine_labels': engine_labels,
        'docker_swarm_node_leader': leader,
        'docker_swarm_node_manager_addr': manager_addr,
        'docker_swarm_node_reachability': reachability,
    }
    if verbose_output:
        hostvars['docker_swarm_node_attributes'] = node

    self._set_composite_vars(self.get_option('compose'), hostvars, hostname, strict=strict)
    self._add_host_to_composed_groups(self.get_option('groups'), hostvars, hostname, strict=strict)
    self._add_host_to_keyed_groups(self.get_option('keyed_groups'), hostvars, hostname, strict=strict)

def verify_file(self, path):
    if super(InventoryModule, self).verify_file(path):
        if path.endswith(('docker_swarm.yml', 'docker_swarm.yaml')):
            return True
    display.debug("docker_swarm inventory filename must end with 'docker_swarm.yml' or 'docker_swarm.yaml'")
    return False

def parse(self, inventory, loader, path, cache=True):
    super(InventoryModule, self).parse(inventory, loader, path)

    self._read_config_data(path)
    self._get_docker_client()
    self._get_swarm_nodes()
    self._populate()

<antml:parameter name="command">python3 -c "
import ast, sys

def get_plugin_shape(filepath):
with open(filepath) as f:
tree = ast.parse(f.read())

result = {}
# Top-level string constants
for node in ast.iter_child_nodes(tree):
    if isinstance(node, ast.Assign):
        for t in node.targets:
            if isinstance(t, ast.Name) and t.id in ('DOCUMENTATION', 'EXAMPLES'):
                result[t.id] = True

# Class
for node in ast.walk(tree):
    if isinstance(node, ast.ClassDef) and node.name == 'InventoryModule':
        bases = [getattr(b, 'attr', getattr(b, 'id', '?')) for b in node.bases]
        result['bases'] = bases
        methods = [n.name for n in ast.iter_child_nodes(node) if isinstance(n, ast.FunctionDef)]
        result['has_parse'] = 'parse' in methods
        result['has_verify_file'] = 'verify_file' in methods
        # Check for NAME attribute
        for n in ast.iter_child_nodes(node):
            if isinstance(n, ast.Assign):
                for t in n.targets:
                    if isinstance(t, ast.Name) and t.id == 'NAME':
                        result['NAME'] = True
return result

our = get_plugin_shape('lib/ansible/plugins/inventory/docker_swarm.py')
ref_hcloud = get_plugin_shape('lib/ansible/plugins/inventory/hcloud.py')
ref_ec2 = get_plugin_shape('lib/ansible/plugins/inventory/aws_ec2.py')
ref_linode = get_plugin_shape('lib/ansible/plugins/inventory/linode.py')

print('docker_swarm:', our)
print('hcloud: ', ref_hcloud)
print('aws_ec2: ', ref_ec2)
print('linode: ', ref_linode)
"</antml:parameter>
<antml:parameter name="description">Compare plugin structure against reference plugins</antml:parameter>
</antml:invoke>
</antml:function_calls>

195 except TLSParameterError as exc:

211 # individual file paths as the Docker CLI and module_utils do.
212 for attr, filename in [('cert_path', 'cert.pem'),
213 ('key_path', 'key.pem'),
214 ('cacert_path', 'ca.pem')]:
215 value = locals().get(attr)
216 if value and os.path.isdir(value):
217 locals()[attr] # noqa – just to clarify the intent
218 if attr == 'cert_path':
219 cert_path = os.path.join(value, filename)
220 elif attr == 'key_path':
221 key_path = os.path.join(value, filename)
222 elif attr == 'cacert_path':
223 cacert_path = os.path.join(value, filename)
224
225 if tls_verify and tls:
226 self._fail("tls and tls_verify are mutually exclusive.")
227
228 if tls or tls_verify:
229 docker_host = docker_host.replace('tcp://', 'https://')
230
231 if tls and cert_path and key_path:
232 tls_config = self._get_tls_config(client_cert=(cert_path, key_path),
233 verify=False,
234 ssl_version=ssl_version)
235 return dict(base_url=docker_host, tls=tls_config,
236 version=api_version, timeout=timeout)
237

240 ssl_version=ssl_version)

246 tls_config = self._get_tls_config(
